testcases.input: nums1 = [2,1,3], nums2 = [10,2,5,0] Output: 13 Explanation: A possible nums3 array is [8,0,7,2,11,3,4,1,9,1,6,3]. The bitwise XOR of all these numbers is 13, so we return 13.
testcases.expectedOutput: 13 Explanation: A possible nums3 array is [8,0,7,2,11,3,4,1,9,1,6,3]. The bitwise XOR of all these numbers is 13, so we return 13.

testcases.input: nums1 = [1,2], nums2 = [3,4] Output: 0 Explanation: All possible pairs of bitwise XORs are nums1[0] ^ nums2[0], nums1[0] ^ nums2[1], nums1[1] ^ nums2[0], and nums1[1] ^ nums2[1]. Thus, one possible nums3 array is [2,5,1,6]. 2 ^ 5 ^ 1 ^ 6 = 0, so we return 0.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: All possible pairs of bitwise XORs are nums1[0] ^ nums2[0], nums1[0] ^ nums2[1], nums1[1] ^ nums2[0], and nums1[1] ^ nums2[1]. Thus, one possible nums3 array is [2,5,1,6]. 2 ^ 5 ^ 1 ^ 6 = 0, so we return 0.
